verdict: One Facebook campaign ran this period with $1,831 spent and no lead or conversion data to evaluate performance.
diagnosis: Spend collapsed from $16.50 daily average to $2.90 on the latest day, suggesting the PCOS and Postpartum campaign is winding down or paused.

signals.headline: Total spend this period: $1,831
signals.detail: All budget routed through one Facebook lead campaign targeting PCOS and postpartum personas. No diversification across audiences or channels.

signals.headline: Daily spend dropped 82% to $2.90
signals.detail: Latest day spend fell far below the $16.50 period average. Campaign appears to be tapering off or hitting a budget cap.

signals.headline: Zero lead or conversion data
signals.detail: No CRM, organic, or local data flowing in. Impossible to judge cost per lead or whether spend produced members.

signals.headline: 224,886 impressions delivered
signals.detail: Reach is healthy for a niche persona campaign. Whether those eyes turned into bookings remains unknown without pipeline data.

benchmark.label: Benchmark
benchmark.subheader: Fitness cost per lead sits near $29 on Meta
benchmark.body: Fitness studios on Meta average around $29.70 per lead in 2026. Boutique studios typically run $25 to $50 per lead. Conversion rates on Meta lead forms reach 14% in fitness — the highest of any vertical.

seasonal.label: Seasonal
seasonal.subheader: June is a cheap lead month
seasonal.body: Fitness cost per lead hit $17 in June 2026, roughly 51% below the global Meta benchmark. April through June is the cheapest stretch of the year for fitness lead acquisition.

competitor.label: Competitor
competitor.body: Boutique operators are moving away from acquisition-heavy spending. Cancellations dropped 6% at studios last year while gyms rose 8%. Pilates, recovery, and community programming are pulling the most demand in 2026.

recommendations.title: Connect CRM and booking data to the dashboard
recommendations.detail: Without lead counts or trial bookings, the $1,831 spent this period cannot be judged. Consider wiring up pipeline data before the next reporting cycle.

recommendations.title: Test holding daily spend steady through June
recommendations.detail: Fitness lead costs are at their yearly low this month. Worth exploring whether restoring the $16 daily pace captures cheap leads before July prices rise.

recommendations.title: Test a second creative angle beyond PCOS and postpartum
recommendations.detail: One persona carries all the risk. Consider testing a low-barrier offer like a free week pass or intro pack to broaden reach without abandoning the niche.
